agent_updates.title: The always-on background monitor that flags anything needing a decision
Rebound Matchup Adjustments Implemented
agent_updates.body_markdown:
> **The Finding:** We were missing the mark on rebounds because we were completely ignoring head-to-head size differences and who is actually boxing out who.

**The Fix:** I built the **Rebound Matchup Model**. It uses the new matchups feed feed to dynamically fetch the expected defender for every player on the slate. It scales each player's proj_reb based on two things: 1) the defender's historical rebound rate, and 2) the height advantage/disadvantage (in inches).

**Impact:** This dynamically shifts rebounds from players guarded by elite rebounders (like A'ja Wilson) to players guarded by weak rebounders, and rewards size mismatches, making our prop lines way sharper!

agent_updates.title: Minutes Model Finalized: v8 beats v9 on Role-Change
agent_updates.body_markdown:
> **The finding:** We judged the v9 (role-conditional) vs v8 (rolling) minutes models on the games that matter most: players stepping into bigger or different roles (the "tail slice").

**Result:** v8 is **BETTER by 0.86 MAE** on these role-change games. The v9 slot-delta adjustment systematically over-corrected on promoted/demoted players.

**Action:** We have permanently shelved the v9 slot-delta approach (Branch 2: Honest Null) and promoted v8 as the official minutes model.

agent_updates.title: Historical Era Adjustments (2024-2026)
agent_updates.body_markdown:
> **2026 season** represents a distinct era in WNBA officiating. Free Throw Rate skyrocketed from ~0.267 to **0.315**, adding over 3 free throw attempts per team per game.

**Impact on Backtesting:** When backtesting player stat projections (especially PTS) on 2024/2025 data, we must apply a downward era-adjustment for free throw volume to avoid artificially grading our 2026-calibrated models too harshly on historical undershoots.

items.name: Never call the project done — keep pushing until we've tried everything
items.header: Never call the project done — keep pushing until we've tried everything
items.why: A standing rule: the work is only ever paused by usage limits, never declared finished.

items.name: Accuracy report: our predictions vs what actually happened
items.header: Accuracy report: our predictions vs what actually happened
items.why: Plus a what-would-today's-method-have-said replay across 163 past games.

items.name: Save the era-adjustment study across all seasons as an in-depth report
items.header: Save the era-adjustment study across all seasons as an in-depth report
items.why: Account for how scoring and officiating shift from year to year in every backtest.

items.header: Keep already-played games out of upcoming reports

items.name: Lead every player page with impact, honestly labeled
items.header: Lead every player page with impact, honestly labeled
items.why: Impact first, playing-time flags, and no fake precision on thin samples.

items.name: Add a second page of depth to the gameday report
items.header: Add a second page of depth to the gameday report
items.why: More detail behind each matchup on the printable gameday report.

items.name: Smell-test fixes: shooting luck, bench context, thin-sample regression
items.header: Smell-test fixes: shooting luck, bench context, thin-sample regression
items.why: Separate real skill from luck and pull tiny samples toward a sensible baseline.

items.name: Turn social-media injury signals into live prediction inputs
items.header: Turn social-media injury signals into live prediction inputs
items.why: The collector is built; put it on a schedule and wire it into the forecasts.

items.name: Add an official injury feed and learn from past news signals
items.header: Add an official injury feed and learn from past news signals
items.why: So social posts aren't our only source for who is playing tonight.

modeling.name: Make foul trouble a live part of the game simulation
modeling.header: Make foul trouble a live part of the game simulation
modeling.why: Fold real foul situations into the possession engine instead of a flat point nudge.

modeling.name: Guard against a broken rating build burying a star player
modeling.header: Guard against a broken rating build burying a star player
modeling.why: Add a safety check so an empty rating build can never hide the best players.

modeling.name: Point displayed prediction ranges at the better-tuned widths
modeling.header: Point displayed prediction ranges at the better-tuned widths
modeling.why: So our stated confidence lines up with how often we are actually right.

modeling.name: Correct the remaining teams we still rate too high or too low
modeling.header: Correct the remaining teams we still rate too high or too low
modeling.why: Dallas and Las Vegas read too low, Los Angeles too high; extend the shipped fix.

modeling.name: Make the win-percent and the point-spread tell the same story
modeling.header: Make the win-percent and the point-spread tell the same story
modeling.why: They currently disagree by about a point; reconcile the two cleanly.

modeling.name: Stop an automatic tune-up from over-correcting the home-court edge
modeling.header: Stop an automatic tune-up from over-correcting the home-court edge
modeling.why: It is about two games from firming too hard; keep the well-calibrated setting.

modeling.name: Golden State expansion-team strength prior — shipped live
modeling.header: Golden State expansion-team strength prior — shipped live
modeling.why: Sharply cut the bias on the new team's games with a market-anchored prior.

modeling.name: Portland expansion-team strength prior — shipped live
modeling.header: Portland expansion-team strength prior — shipped live
modeling.why: Stacked on the Golden State fix for another big bias reduction.

modeling.name: Grade our season simulation against the futures market
modeling.header: Grade our season simulation against the futures market
modeling.why: Title, playoff and win-total odds scored vs the market — the sim is the product.

infra.name: Build a usage-and-budget report for the automated work
infra.header: Build a usage-and-budget report for the automated work
infra.why: See spend at a glance and re-slot the model-building work against it.

infra.name: Recover and re-run a request if a session crashes mid-work
infra.header: Recover and re-run a request if a session crashes mid-work
infra.why: A low-priority safety net so a crash never quietly drops your message.

infra.name: Stop cutting off long messages when they are captured
infra.header: Stop cutting off long messages when they are captured
infra.why: Raise the capture limit so no request tail is ever lost at intake again.

infra.name: Let batched answers wait for the morning digest without churning
infra.header: Let batched answers wait for the morning digest without churning
infra.why: A non-blocking state so the reminder system does not thrash on a held answer.

infra.name: Only spin up extra work lanes when the next item clears a value bar
infra.header: Only spin up extra work lanes when the next item clears a value bar
infra.why: Stops overnight sessions from over-spending on low-value tail work.

infra.name: Reserve the heavy independent double-check for work that ships
infra.header: Reserve the heavy independent double-check for work that ships
infra.why: Dead-end results get a light self-check; save the deep check for live changes.

infra.name: Always merge onto the latest code, never blind-copy a work branch
infra.header: Always merge onto the latest code, never blind-copy a work branch
infra.why: Prevents a stale branch from silently reverting a live feature.

infra.name: Lock down messaging, workload-width and board-freeze safeguards
infra.header: Lock down messaging, workload-width and board-freeze safeguards
infra.why: Root-cause fixes so only one voice sends and the workload stays honest.

infra.name: One-time code-publishing sign-in to keep pushes flowing
infra.header: One-time code-publishing sign-in to keep pushes flowing
infra.why: Needs a human sign-in; mostly self-heals now, with a small residual.

upcoming.note: These are the next things the system will work on — what each will be and why it matters. Tap to move an item to the top, bump it up, push it down, or shelf it. Items waiting on your call are parked until you answer — then they jump to the top.

rollover.label: rollover armed for tomorrow ✓
rollover.note: After 4am ET the board flips to the new day, files yesterday's reports under a dated header, and shows tonight's placeholder until the reports land at 5:15pm ET.
